LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART. DEFENDANT DROPBOX INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IN THE OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF DID NOT OPPOSES THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED THAT THERE WAS AN AGREEMENT WHICH ENTITLED HIM TO ATTORNEY'S FEES, AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED THAT HE IS ENTITLED TO ATTORNEY'S FEES UNDER A STATUTE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS THAT SHOW MALICIOUS CONDUCT ON DEFENDANT DROPBOX INC.'S PART OR THAT DEFENDANT DROPBOX INC. HAD PRIOR KNOWLEDGE OF DEFENDANT ARTHUR JOSEPHSON'S PROPENSITY FOR BECOMING VIOLENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. A DEFENDANT CAN BE HELD LIABLE FOR A PREMISE LIABILITY CLAIM ARISING FROM A THIRD-PARTY'S TORTIOUS OR CRIMINAL CONDUCT. FRANCES T. V. VILLAGE GREEN OWNERS ASSN. (1986) 42 CAL.3D 490; SEE SAATZER V. SMITH (1981) 122 CAL.APP.3D 512.) DEFENDANT DROPBOX INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS WHICH INDICATE THAT DEFENDANT DROPBOX INC. HAD PRIOR KNOWLEDGE OF DEFENDANT ARTHUR JOSEPHSON'S PROPENSITY FOR BECOMING VIOLENT WHILE INTOXICATED OR THAT DEFENDANT DROPBOX HAD AN OPPORTUNITY TO INTERVENE AND PREVENT DEFENDANT ARTHUR JOSEPHSON'S VIOLENT CONDUCT BUT FAILED TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARTHUR JOSEPHSON'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. WHILE STATUTORY AND CASE LAW PERMITS THE FILING OF THE DEMURRER, IN THE CONTEXT OF THIS CASE NO USEFUL PURPOSE IS SERVED BY ITS FILING OR BY SUSTAINING IT IN WHOLE OR IN PART. MR. JOSEPHSON SHOULD SERVE DISCOVERY DIRECTED TO THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-14-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC. Motion For Protective Order is taken off calendar. Dismissal with prejudice of entire action of all parties and all causes of action filed by Plaintiff on July 1, 2016. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2016 Set For Trial On Feb-06-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint Against Arthur Josephson
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service And Entering Dismissal And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Continued To Jun-22-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint Against Arthur Josephson
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service And Entering Dismissal And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-25-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT JONATHAN YING'S Motion For Order Quashing Service And Entering Dismissal And Request For Sanctions is continued to May 10, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion, per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Mar-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC.'S Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages allegations is denied. The second amended complaint adequately alleges that Dropbox had prior knowledge of Mr. Josephson's alleged propensity to be violent after consuming alcohol, which is sufficient to support plaintiff's request for punitive damages. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ARTHUR JOSEPHSON'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. While statutory and case law permits the filing of the demurrer, in the context of this case no useful purpose is served by its filing or by sustaining it in whole or in part. Mr. Josephson should serve discovery directed to the affirmative defenses. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2016 Continued To Apr-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Knight of Murchison & Cumming LLP telephone number415-524-4300 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend in part and sustained with leave to amend in part. Defendant Dropbox Inc.'s motion to strike Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is sustained without leave to amend. In the opposition, Plaintiff did not opposes the motion to strike the request for attorney's fees, Plaintiff has not alleged that there was an agreement which entitled him to attorney's fees, and Plaintiff has not alleged that he is entitled to attorney's fees under a statute. The motion to strike the request for punitive damages is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, because Plaintiff has not alleged facts that show malicious conduct on Defendant Dropbox Inc.'s part or that Defendant Dropbox Inc. had prior knowledge of Defendant Arthur Josephson's propensity for becoming violent. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Knight of Murchison & Cumming LLP telephone number415-524-4300 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. A defendant can be held liable for a premise liability claim arising from a third-party's tortious or criminal conduct. Frances T. v. Village Green Owners Assn. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 490; See Saatzer v. Smith (1981) 122 Cal.App.3d 512.) Defendant Dropbox Inc.'s demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, because Plaintiff has not alleged facts which indicate that Defendant Dropbox Inc. had prior knowledge of Defendant Arthur Josephson's propensity for becoming violent while intoxicated or that Defendant Dropbox had an opportunity to intervene and prevent Defendant Arthur Josephson's violent conduct but failed to do so. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Continued To Feb-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Dropbox Inc Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Regarding The Deposition Of Jonathan Ying
Off Calendar Dec-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. AUDREY C. SIEGEL, ESQ. of Anderson Law, Tel No. 415-395-9898 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. THOMAS W.J. PURTELL, ESQ. of Law Offices of Thomas W.J. Purtell, Tel No. 415-722-6291 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Arthur Josephson.

Both sides stipulate to matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling (with the one addition of setting a deadline re: available deposition dates): DEFENDANT DROPBOX, INC.'S Motion For Protective Order Regarding The Deposition Of Jonathan Ying. Motion denied. The Court finds that Liberty Mutual v. Superior Court is inapplicable in this situation. First, Mr. Ying is not a corporate executive analogous to the deponents at issue in Liberty Mutual. Second, it appears that Mr. Ying was in charge of Drop Box's social events, and as such, may have knowledge regarding Drop Box's policies and procedures on these events and the conduct required of employees. If Mr. Ying has no knowledge of whether Plaintiff was acting within the scope of his employment during the incident at the issue, he can state that at his deposition. If the questioning of Mr. Ying becomes harassing or intrudes on privileged matters, Defendant retains the right to suspend the deposition and move for a protective order. Sanctions denied. Counsel for Defendant/Cross-Defendant Dropbox, Inc., must provide all parties by December 9, 2015, three (3) dates in which Jonathan Ying is available for deposition before January 15, 2016. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
